title: Lesson 414
transcript: Hi, one of the things that security has to do is obviously support the safety of personnel within the organization on a regular basis, regardless of where those employees are. may be acting, for example, on behalf of the organization itself. So here's a section that deals with exactly that. And again, there's always value in understanding and pausing to think about the words that come directly from the exam outline. So 7.15 is focused on addressing personnel safety and security concerns. And what's listed specifically in the exam outline in relation to this and how the security function needs to be able to support people, for example, and personnel and employees that may be traveling, for example, but also keep them safe and also emergency management. This is why, for example, we have fire drills and earthquake drills and so on so that people can be aware of what they need to be able to do in the event of any sort of emergency happening. And also what's referred to as duress or employee duress. This is where there could be, for example, violent pressure that is put on your employees and having them be able to react accordingly. with obviously safety in mind is very important. And when it comes to duress, there could be all kinds of situations where employees are being put under pressure to do things that they wouldn't normally want to do or puts them at risk, for example. And it's not uncommon for people to know some key word, for example, that they can relay to the organization and security and so on to be able to alert the security function and others that they may be under duress and therefore security has to be able to act in those situations. The whole point of this is that employees need to be protected in whatever capacity they may be in, whether they're traveling or acting on behalf of the organization in some other capacity and so on. And what's important is for security to provide awareness, training and education so that employees can feel safe, but also be able to react when they are put into situations that may put them at risk.
